pattern: Each output cell is the floor of the average over the cell and its existing neighbors in a 3x3 window, clamped to the grid edges.
hints: For every cell, look at the up-to-nine cells in its surrounding 3x3 block, skipping any that fall outside the grid.
Sum the valid neighbors and divide by how many you actually counted, not always nine.
Use integer floor division and write results into a fresh grid so updates do not affect later cells.

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(50, size)))
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(50, size)))
    grid = [[rng.randint(0, 255) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]
    return [grid]
